        - generic [ref=e51]:
          - heading "Playwright Test" [level=3] [ref=e52]
          - paragraph [ref=e53]: Full-featured test runner with auto-waiting, assertions, tracing, and parallelism across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.
          - code [ref=e54]: npm init playwright@latest
          - link "Testing documentation" [ref=e56] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /docs/intro
        - generic [ref=e57]:
          - heading "Playwright CLI" [level=3] [ref=e58]
          - paragraph [ref=e59]: Token-efficient browser automation for coding agents like Claude Code and GitHub Copilot. Skill-based workflows without large context overhead.
          - code [ref=e60]: npm i -g @playwright/cli@latest
          - link "CLI documentation" [ref=e62] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /docs/getting-started-cli
        - generic [ref=e63]:
          - heading "Playwright MCP" [level=3] [ref=e64]
          - paragraph [ref=e65]: Model Context Protocol server that gives AI agents full browser control through structured accessibility snapshots.
          - code [ref=e66]: npx @playwright/mcp@latest
          - link "MCP documentation" [ref=e68] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /docs/getting-started-mcp
      - generic [ref=e70]:
        - heading "Built for testing" [level=2] [ref=e71]
        - generic [ref=e73]:
          - generic [ref=e74]:
            - heading "Auto-wait and web-first assertions" [level=4] [ref=e75]
            - paragraph [ref=e76]: Playwright waits for elements to be actionable before performing actions. Assertions automatically retry until conditions are met. No artificial timeouts, no flaky tests.
            - heading "Test isolation" [level=4] [ref=e77]
            - paragraph [ref=e78]: Each test gets a fresh browser context — equivalent to a brand new browser profile. Full isolation with near-zero overhead. Save authentication state once and reuse it across tests.
          - generic [ref=e79]:
            - heading "Resilient locators" [level=4] [ref=e80]
            - paragraph [ref=e81]:
              - text: "Find elements with selectors that mirror how users see the page:"
              - code [ref=e82]: getByRole
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e83]: getByLabel
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e84]: getByPlaceholder
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e85]: getByTestId
              - text: . No brittle CSS paths.
            - heading "Parallelism and sharding" [level=4] [ref=e86]
            - paragraph [ref=e87]: Tests run in parallel by default across all configured browsers. Shard across multiple machines for faster CI. Full cross-browser coverage on every commit.
      - generic [ref=e89]:
        - heading "Built for AI agents" [level=2] [ref=e90]
        - generic [ref=e92]:
          - generic [ref=e93]:
            - heading "Accessibility snapshots, not screenshots" [level=4] [ref=e94]
            - paragraph [ref=e95]: Agents interact with pages through structured accessibility trees — element roles, names, and refs. Deterministic and unambiguous, no vision models required.
            - heading "MCP server" [level=4] [ref=e96]
            - paragraph [ref=e97]:
              - text: Drop-in
              - link "Model Context Protocol" [ref=e98] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://modelcontextprotocol.io
              - text: server for VS Code, Cursor, Claude Desktop, Windsurf, and any MCP client. Full browser control through standard tool calls.
          - generic [ref=e99]:
            - heading "CLI for coding agents" [level=4] [ref=e100]
            - paragraph [ref=e101]: Token-efficient command-line interface with installable skills. Purpose-built for Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and similar coding agents that need to balance browser automation with large codebases.
            - heading "Session monitoring" [level=4] [ref=e102]
            - paragraph [ref=e103]: Visual dashboard with live screencast previews of all running browser sessions. Click any session to zoom in and take control.
      - generic [ref=e105]:

extraVariables.repo_profile:
# Repo profile (extracted — authoritative)

auth:
  auth_fixture_name: null
  global_setup_path: null
  storage_state_path: null
notes: >
  The tests primarily use the Page Object Model (POM) style for organizing page
  interactions.

  There is a consistent use of `getByRole` for locators, but no test ID
  attributes are utilized.

  The filename convention is kebab-case, and the spec files have a `.spec.ts`
  suffix.

  No custom fixtures or authentication mechanisms are detected in the current
  setup.
imports:
  reexports_test: false
  test_import_source: "@playwright/test"
fixtures:
  custom_fixtures: []
locators:
  primary_pattern: getByRole
  per_subdirectory: []
  test_id_attribute: null
structure:
  spec_suffix: .spec.ts
  page_object_dir: tests/pages
  page_object_style: pom_class
  filename_convention: kebab-case
  page_object_base_class: null
assertions:
  custom_matchers: []
  poll_pattern_used: true
  soft_assertions_used: false
  visual_snapshots_used: false
playwright:
  projects:
    - chromium
  test_dir: ./tests
  base_url_env: PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL
  version_detected: unknown
  web_server_configured: false
conventions_confidence: 0.9
extraVariables.related_sources: (none — stack trace stayed within the spec and page object)
extraVariables.prior_feedback:
1 previous patch rejected by a human reviewer.

Most instructive verdicts:
- REJECTED tests/promodemo/promo.spec.ts (locator_drift): "Get Started failures here are PROD-4499, a known product bug — do not patch, refuse these"

REJECTED notes are corrections from a human who saw a previous patch fail in this repo. Treat them as constraints — do not repeat those mistakes.
